summary: Official Microsoft policy for recommended troubleshooting. The app now writes only the documented TroubleshootingAllowRecommendations=0 policy value and no longer carries the extra WindowsMitigation companion write.

setting.name: Recommended Troubleshooting for Known Problems
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can automatically apply or suggest troubleshooting for known problems. This policy controls how much of that automation is allowed.
setting.why_people_change_it: People lock it down when they do not want Windows or Microsoft automatically applying troubleshooting, or when they want a predictable managed-device support policy.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Lower values reduce automatic troubleshooting and notifications. This now maps to the official six-value policy that Microsoft documents.
setting.professional_notes: MSDT.admx documents TroubleshootingAllowRecommendations under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Troubleshooting\AllowRecommendations with values 0 through 5. The app now writes only the documented policy value 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: The user can configure how troubleshooting is applied.

allowed_values.label: Do nothing
allowed_values.meaning: Do not allow users, system features, or Microsoft to apply troubleshooting.

allowed_values.label: Apply critical only
allowed_values.meaning: Only automatically apply troubleshooting for critical problems by system features and Microsoft.

allowed_values.label: Ask first for other problems
allowed_values.meaning: Automatically apply critical troubleshooting and notify users about troubleshooting for other problems.

allowed_values.label: Apply and notify
allowed_values.meaning: Automatically apply troubleshooting for critical and other problems and notify users when troubleshooting solved a problem.

allowed_values.label: Apply silently
allowed_values.meaning: Automatically apply troubleshooting for critical and other problems without notifying users when it solved a problem.

allowed_values.label: Defer to user
allowed_values.meaning: Allow the user to choose their own troubleshooting settings.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes only the documented policy value 0 under HKLM\Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Troubleshooting\AllowRecommendations.

windows_defaults.label: Windows device-policy default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows 10 version 1903 and newer systems exposing recommended troubleshooting policy

states.rationale: Microsoft documents that if the policy is not configured, the user can configure how troubleshooting is applied.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: Systems that want the normal troubleshooting settings flow
avoid_for: Managed environments that want to hard-block recommended troubleshooting
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want users to keep control over recommended troubleshooting behavior.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want to force a strict no-troubleshooting policy.
tradeoffs: Users can keep using the normal troubleshooting settings flow.
No hard managed-policy restriction is applied.

recommended_profiles.label: Candidate do nothing
intended_for: Managed devices that want troubleshooting blocked
avoid_for: Anyone expecting a different troubleshooting mode
recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to block recommended troubleshooting through the official policy.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a different troubleshooting mode.
tradeoffs: The official policy meaning is clear.
The app now matches the documented policy surface.

evidence.title: Microsoft Troubleshooting Policy CSP: TroubleshootingAllowRecommendations
evidence.summary: Official documentation for the recommended troubleshooting policy, including the documented values 0 through 5 and the not-configured behavior.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft MSDT.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file defines TroubleshootingAllowRecommendations under Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\Troubleshooting\AllowRecommendations with values 0 through 5.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft MSDT.adml help text
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text explains the six documented troubleshooting modes and says that not configuring the policy leaves the user in control.

decision.why: The official policy is well documented and the app now writes only the documented policy value 0.

validation_proof.notes: The current app now writes only the documented policy value 0.
